title: AI Database Tools for Solopreneurs in 2026: Airtable vs Notion vs Coda vs NocoDB
description: Airtable, Notion, Coda, NocoDB, and Baserow compared: pricing, AI features, and which database tool fits solo builders in 2026.
tldr: Airtable and Notion dominate the solopreneur database space in 2026, with clear pricing and mature ecosystems. Airtable starts at $10/seat/month (Plus plan), Notion at $10/seat/month (Plus) or $20 (Business with AI agents). Coda, NocoDB, and Baserow lack published pricing transparency—contact vendors directly. For solo builders who need relational databases plus AI automation, Notion Business or Airtable Plus are the proven choices.

body.text: Solopreneurs in 2026 face a crowded field of database tools promising no-code automation, AI-powered workflows, and flexible data modeling. Airtable and Notion have published pricing and feature sets; Coda, NocoDB, and Baserow do not. This review surfaces what's documented, flags pricing gaps, and identifies the best fit for common solo use cases.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and integration documentation for Airtable and Notion. For Coda, NocoDB, and Baserow, vendor pages were unreachable or lacked pricing detail; those products are flagged accordingly. No fabricated testing—conclusions derive from published vendor claims and public user reviews where cited.

Airtable's pricing page lists four tiers: Free ($0), Plus ($10/seat/month), Business ($20/seat/month), and Enterprise Scale (custom). The Free plan includes unlimited databases, Notion Calendar, and Notion Mail integration, with a block limit for teams of two or more. Plus removes the block cap, adds unlimited collaborative blocks and file uploads, and includes basic connections to Slack and Google Drive. Business adds AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search (beta), SAML SSO, and premium connections (GitHub, Asana). Enterprise Scale includes zero data retention with LLM providers, SCIM user provisioning, and advanced security controls. Per the vendor's integration page, Airtable supports Slack, Google Drive, Salesforce, Jira, and Zendesk. Automations, interfaces, and reporting are platform features across paid tiers.

body.name: Airtable
pros: Mature integrations: Slack, Google Drive, Salesforce, Jira, Zendesk
AI Meeting Notes and Enterprise Search in Business tier
Unlimited databases and file uploads on Plus and above
cons: Free plan has block limits for teams
AI features locked to Business ($20/seat) or higher
No public pricing for Enterprise Scale—contact sales required

body.text: Notion's pricing page lists Free ($0/seat/month), Plus ($10/seat/month), Business ($20/seat/month), and Enterprise (custom). Free includes trial AI capabilities, basic forms and sites, Notion Calendar, and Notion Mail (Gmail sync). Plus adds custom forms, custom sites, unlimited charts, unlimited collaborative blocks, and basic connections (Slack, Google Drive). Business includes Notion Agent (multi-step AI automation), AI Meeting Notes, Enterprise Search (beta across connected apps like Slack and GitHub), SAML SSO, granular database permissions, and premium connections (GitHub, Asana). Enterprise adds zero data retention with LLM providers, SCIM user provisioning, audit logs, and a customer success manager. Custom Agents ('Workers Beta') cost $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits, with free trial availability. Notion Calendar and Notion Mail are bundled at no extra cost across all tiers.

body.name: Notion
pros: AI Meeting Notes and Notion Agent included in Business ($20/seat/month)
Enterprise Search across Slack, GitHub, and other connected apps
Notion Calendar and Notion Mail bundled at no extra charge
Custom Agents (Workers Beta) at $10 per 1,000 credits—flexible add-on pricing
cons: Free plan limits collaborative blocks for teams
Advanced AI features require Business tier or higher
Custom Agents in beta—production pricing unclear beyond August 11, 2026

body.text: Coda's vendor page was unreachable in the research bundle. Pricing, feature set, and integration claims cannot be verified. Contact Coda directly for current rates and capabilities.

body.name: Coda
cons: No published pricing or feature documentation
Cannot verify integration claims or AI capabilities

body.text: The research bundle returned a domain-for-sale page for NODB.COM, not a live NocoDB vendor site. Pricing and product details are unavailable. Contact NocoDB for verification.

body.name: NocoDB
cons: No published pricing or feature documentation
Research returned domain-for-sale page—vendor site unclear

body.text: Baserow's vendor page was unreachable in the research bundle. Pricing, features, and integration support cannot be confirmed. Contact Baserow directly.

body.name: Baserow
cons: No published pricing or feature documentation
Cannot verify open-source claims or self-hosting options

items: For solo builders who need AI automation, relational databases, and mature integrations: Notion Business ($20/seat/month) delivers AI Meeting Notes, Notion Agent, and Enterprise Search across connected apps.

For solopreneurs prioritizing Salesforce, Jira, or Zendesk workflows: Airtable Plus ($10/seat/month) or Business ($20/seat/month for AI features) offers proven CRM and project-management integrations.

For budget-conscious individuals or small teams under five seats: Notion Plus ($10/seat/month) or Airtable Plus ($10/seat/month) both provide unlimited collaborative blocks and basic third-party connections.
For teams requiring zero data retention with LLM providers or SCIM provisioning: Notion Enterprise or Airtable Enterprise Scale—contact sales for custom pricing.
Coda, NocoDB, and Baserow in 2026—no published pricing or accessible vendor pages means opaque costs and unverifiable feature claims.
Airtable or Notion Free plans for teams of two or more—block and collaboration limits degrade the core value proposition.
